name: Kansas SNAP Food Assistance
description: Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program providing monthly food benefits via EBT card to eligible low-income Kansas households.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals and families meeting federal income and resource guidelines
incomeLimitNotes: Gross income at or below 130% FPL; net income at or below 100% FPL

name: KanCare (Kansas Medicaid)
description: Kansas Medicaid program providing comprehensive health coverage through managed care organizations for low-income children, families, pregnant women, elderly, and disabled residents.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income children, pregnant women, elderly, and disabled individuals
incomeLimitNotes: Children up to 170% FPL; pregnant women up to 171% FPL; aged/disabled at SSI levels

name: Kansas Department of Labor - Unemployment Insurance
description: Provides temporary financial assistance to Kansas workers who lost their jobs through no fault of their own while they search for new employment.
eligibilitySummary: Workers who lost employment through no fault of their own and meet wage requirements
incomeLimitNotes: Must have earned sufficient wages during the base period

name: Kansas Housing Resources Corporation (KHRC)
description: State housing finance agency administering affordable housing programs including rental assistance, homebuyer programs, and homelessness prevention across Kansas.
eligibilitySummary: Low to moderate income individuals and families seeking affordable housing
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program; generally up to 80% area median income

name: Kansas Legal Services
description: Provides free civil legal assistance to low-income Kansans in areas including family law, housing, consumer protection, public benefits, and elder law.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income Kansas residents with civil legal needs
incomeLimitNotes: Generally at or below 125% of federal poverty level

name: Kansas Works - Workforce Centers
description: State workforce development system with centers across Kansas providing job search assistance, resume help, career counseling, and training programs.
eligibilitySummary: Any Kansas resident seeking employment or career development services

name: Kansas LIHEAP - Energy Assistance
description: Low Income Home Energy Assistance Program helping eligible Kansas households pay heating and cooling bills through the Department for Children and Families.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income households needing help with energy costs
incomeLimitNotes: Household income at or below 150% of federal poverty level

name: Kansas 211
description: Free statewide information and referral service connecting Kansans with local health and human services resources available around the clock.
eligibilitySummary: Any Kansas resident seeking help or information about available services

name: Kansas Food Bank
description: Feeding America affiliate serving central and western Kansas distributing food to over 500 partner agencies including food pantries, shelters, and soup kitchens.
eligibilitySummary: Individuals and families in need of food assistance in central and western Kansas

name: Harvesters - The Community Food Network
description: Feeding America affiliate serving northeast Kansas and northwest Missouri distributing food through a network of pantries, kitchens, and shelters.
eligibilitySummary: Individuals and families in need of food assistance in northeast Kansas

name: Kansas Commission on Veterans' Affairs
description: Assists Kansas veterans and their families with accessing state and federal benefits including claims assistance, education, employment, and the Kansas Veterans Home.
eligibilitySummary: Kansas veterans and their families

name: Kansas Suicide Prevention HQ
description: Statewide crisis resource connecting individuals to the 988 Suicide and Crisis Lifeline and providing mental health crisis resources for Kansans.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in Kansas experiencing a mental health crisis, suicidal thoughts, or emotional distress

name: Kansas Child Care Assistance Program
description: Subsidized childcare program helping low-income working families and families in training afford quality childcare for their children.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income working families with children under 13 needing childcare
incomeLimitNotes: Family income at or below 85% of state median income

name: Kansas WIC Program
description: Women, Infants, and Children nutrition program providing supplemental foods, nutrition education, and health referrals for eligible women and young children.
eligibilitySummary: Pregnant, breastfeeding, and postpartum women; infants and children under 5
incomeLimitNotes: Income at or below 185% of federal poverty level

name: Kansas TANF Cash Assistance
description: Temporary Assistance for Needy Families providing cash benefits and support services to very low-income families with dependent children in Kansas.
eligibilitySummary: Very low-income families with dependent children
incomeLimitNotes: Income must be below TANF payment standard for family size

name: Kansas Vocational Rehabilitation Services
description: Provides employment-related services to Kansans with disabilities including job training, assistive technology, job placement, and support services.
eligibilitySummary: Kansas residents with physical or mental disabilities that impact their ability to work

name: Wichita Transit
description: Public bus system serving the Wichita metropolitan area with fixed routes, paratransit services, and reduced fares for eligible riders.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in the Wichita area; reduced fare for seniors, disabled, and Medicare cardholders

name: Kansas City Kansas Housing Authority
description: Provides public housing and Housing Choice Vouchers to low-income families, elderly, and disabled residents in Wyandotte County.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals and families in Kansas City, Kansas area
incomeLimitNotes: Income at or below 80% area median income for public housing; 50% for vouchers

name: Kansas Weatherization Assistance Program
description: Provides free home energy efficiency improvements to low-income Kansas households including insulation, air sealing, and heating system upgrades.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income homeowners and renters
incomeLimitNotes: Household income at or below 200% of federal poverty level

name: Kansas Adult Education
description: Free adult basic education, GED preparation, and English language classes offered through community colleges and learning centers statewide.
eligibilitySummary: Adults age 16 and older not enrolled in K-12 school

name: Kansas Domestic Violence Hotline
description: Statewide hotline operated by the Kansas Coalition Against Sexual and Domestic Violence providing crisis intervention, safety planning, and shelter referrals.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone experiencing or at risk of domestic violence or sexual assault in Kansas

name: United Way of the Plains - Wichita Area Services
description: Connects Wichita area residents to a network of funded partner agencies providing services in education, financial stability, and health.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in the Wichita metro area seeking assistance or community resources

name: Kansas Lifeline Telephone Assistance
description: Provides discounted phone and broadband service to eligible low-income Kansans to ensure access to essential telecommunications.
eligibilitySummary: Households participating in SNAP, Medicaid, SSI, or with income at or below 135% FPL
incomeLimitNotes: Income at or below 135% FPL or participation in qualifying federal program

name: Johnson County Transit - Kansas City Suburbs
description: Public transit serving Johnson County suburbs in the Kansas City metro with commuter routes, microtransit, and paratransit options.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in Johnson County; reduced fare for seniors and disabled riders

name: Kansas Health Insurance Marketplace
description: Federal health insurance marketplace where Kansans can shop for qualified health plans with potential premium tax credits and cost-sharing reductions.
eligibilitySummary: Kansans without employer coverage or other qualifying health insurance
incomeLimitNotes: Premium tax credits available for households between 100-400% FPL

name: Wichita Housing Authority
description: Provides public housing and Housing Choice Voucher rental assistance to low-income families, elderly, and disabled residents in the Wichita area.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals and families in the Wichita area
incomeLimitNotes: Income at or below 80% area median income

name: Kansas Department for Aging and Disability Services
description: Provides services for seniors and people with disabilities including home and community-based services, nursing facility care, and support programs.
eligibilitySummary: Kansas seniors age 60+ and individuals with disabilities

name: Kansas Promise Scholarship
description: State scholarship covering tuition and fees at Kansas community colleges and technical colleges for students in eligible high-demand programs.
eligibilitySummary: Kansas residents enrolling in eligible programs at participating institutions

name: El Centro Inc. - Kansas City Kansas
description: Provides comprehensive services for the Latino community in Kansas City including immigration legal services, workforce development, health programs, and family support.
eligibilitySummary: Latino families and immigrants in the Kansas City metro area

name: Kansas Statewide Farmworker Health Program
description: Provides healthcare access and community health services for migrant and seasonal farmworkers and their families across rural Kansas.
eligibilitySummary: Migrant and seasonal farmworkers and their families in Kansas
incomeLimitNotes: Sliding fee scale based on income
